title: Night Garage Shader Workshop
description: The user stands in a dimly lit garage scene where a car with clear-coat paint sits behind a broken glass window, illuminated by a barn lamp. They must interactively tune shader parameters across all three objects—adjusting glass refraction, car paint clear-coat properties, and lamp anisotropy—while obeying an energy conservation law that couples total scene reflectance, with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net. Load three GLB assets: GlassBrokenWindow.glb positioned at (0,1.5,-3) as a wall partition, ClearCoatCarPaint.glb positioned at (0,0.5,-5) behind the window as a parked car, and AnisotropyBarnLamp.glb positioned at (2,3,-1) as the primary scene light source. Use GLTFLoader and set up an OrbitControls camera at (0,2,4) looking toward the car. Add a dim ambient light (0.15 intensity) and a point light co-located with the barn lamp model (warm color #ffcc88, intensity 3, distance 15). Create a GUI panel (use lil-gui from CDN) with these shader controls: (1) Glass IOR slider 1.0-2.5, (2) Glass opacity 0.0-1.0, (3) Glass tint color picker, (4) Car clearCoat intensity 0.0-1.0, (5) Car clearCoatRoughness 0.0-1.0, (6) Car base color picker, (7) Lamp anisotropy strength 0.0-1.0, (8) Lamp anisotropy rotation 0-2PI, (9) Lamp metalness 0.0-1.0, (10) Global energy budget slider 1.0-3.0. Implement an energy conservation law: the sum of glass transmission + car clearCoat + lamp anisotropy must not exceed the global energy budget value. When any parameter changes, clamp the most-recently-unchanged parameters proportionally to satisfy the constraint, and visually flash the clamped slider red briefly. Traverse each loaded GLTF scene and apply MeshPhysicalMaterial overrides matching the controls. Implement a full undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z) that stores snapshots of all 10 parameter values on every change, capped at 50 entries. Display a small HUD showing current energy sum vs budget and undo stack depth. Ensure all materials update in real time when sliders move. Add a subtle environment map using PMREMGenerator with a simple procedural gradient for reflections. The scene should feel moody and atmospheric with visible light caustics on the car paint through the broken glass.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  glassIOR: number,         // index of refraction for broken window glass (1.0-2.5)
  glassOpacity: number,         // opacity of glass material (0.0-1.0)
  carClearCoat: number,         // clear coat intensity on car paint (0.0-1.0)
  carClearCoatRoughness: number,         // roughness of car clear coat layer (0.0-1.0)
  lampAnisotropy: number,         // anisotropic reflection strength on barn lamp (0.0-1.0)
  lampAnisotropyRotation: number,         // rotation angle of anisotropic highlights in radians (0-6.28)
  lampMetalness: number,         // metallic property of lamp material (0.0-1.0)
  globalEnergyBudget: number,         // maximum combined energy for shader effects (1.0-3.0)
  energyViolationCount: number,         // count of times energy conservation was violated
  totalParameterChanges: number,         // total number of shader parameter adjustments made
  assetsLoaded: boolean,         // whether all three GLB models loaded successfully
  guiInitialized: boolean,         // whether the GUI panel is created and functional
}
